gold_final_answer: All pairs of positive integers with n = m or n = 2m.
topics_flat: Discrete Mathematics > Combinatorics > Coloring schemes, extremal arguments
prompt:
Find all pairs of positive integers $(m, n)$, $m \le n$, such that there exists an $m \times n$ table of zeros and ones which satisfy the following condition:
If there is a zero (resp. one) in a cell, then the number of the zeros (resp. ones) in the row of that cell is equal to the number of the zeros (resp. ones) in the column of the cell.
